behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the heuristic algorithm itself (single function/object), not module wiring. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected bogus shortcut: not choosing Integration just because the product is a service; this behavior is isolated algorithm quality. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior text only requires route-length ratio, with no real HTTP/subprocess/OS condition.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function directly on deterministic coordinate sets. For each case, compute/lookup optimal length (exact solver for smaller n plus fixed benchmark cases for larger n) and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for n<=20.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic formula/config mapping logic; failure is in local computation. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: reject "Integration by default for multi-module features"; unless behavior text requires config loading/wiring failures, Unit is sufficient. Rejected higher level: Integration is disqualified because behavior text focuses on correctness of computed estimates from given inputs, not persistence/service boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests over (distance, road_type, speed_config) asserting expected leg duration and aggregated time windows, including rounding and edge values.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Pure validation arithmetic and comparison; failure is local logic. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: reject choosing higher level merely because validator is called by a service; this behavior itself is one function boundary. Rejected higher level: Integration is disqualified because behavior text only requires correct reject/amount computation from provided totals.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test validator with totals below/equal/above shift hours; assert boolean rejection and exact excess-time value (including zero boundary).

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff: geocoding result handling -> filtering -> optimizer input -> skipped-address reporting. Unit test of one function can miss orchestration defects. REAL-INFRA check: behavior says API returns no results (deterministic response), so in-process fake geocoder is sufficient; no need for real HTTP, so not System. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Run service-level test wiring geocode client (stubbed), route optimizer, and reporting modules. Stub one address as no-result, others as valid coordinates. Assert failed address is listed as skipped and final route contains only geocoded stops.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration-brake applied: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no AI output dependence; no multi-agent chain.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Real failure risk is cross-module: dedup/merge logic must propagate into optimizer so route visits once while preserving combined items. Testing only dedup function can miss handoff regressions. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: System disqualified because behavior text does not require real external API or OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Service-level in-process test with duplicate-address inputs containing different item payloads. Assert output route has one stop for that address, merged items are preserved, and visit count for that address is exactly one.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Adversarial critique applied: not escalating to System just because the full service may call APIs elsewhere.
